expectations.text: Specifies the 24-hour early warning to the CSIRT or competent authority, indicating whether the incident is suspected to be caused by unlawful/malicious acts and whether it could have cross-border impact (Art. 23(4)(a))
expectations.evidence: 'Stage 1: Early Warning — Within 24 Hours... What to include: Whether the incident is suspected of being caused by unlawful or malicious action (yes, in this case — ransomware); Whether it could have a cross-border impact.'

expectations.text: Specifies the 72-hour incident notification updating the early warning with an initial assessment of severity and impact and available indicators of compromise (Art. 23(4)(b))
expectations.evidence: 'Stage 2: Incident Notification — Within 72 Hours... An initial assessment of the incident, including its severity and impact; Indicators of compromise (IoCs) where available — file hashes, ransomware variant/family if identified, C2 infrastructure, TTPs observed.'

expectations.text: Specifies the final report within one month containing a detailed description, threat type / root cause, applied and ongoing mitigation, and any cross-border impact (Art. 23(4)(d)) — including the progress-report rule if the incident is still ongoing at the one-month mark
expectations.evidence: 'Stage 4: Final Report... A detailed description...; The type of threat or root cause...; Mitigation measures applied and ongoing; Where applicable, the cross-border impact.' Plus: 'If the incident is still ongoing at the one-month mark, you submit a progress report at that point and a final report once it is actually resolved.'

expectations.text: Explains why this qualifies as a 'significant incident' (Art. 23(3): severe operational disruption or financial loss / considerable damage to others) and mentions intermediate status updates upon authority request (Art. 23(4)(c))
expectations.evidence: 'Step 0' quotes both prongs of the significant-incident test and applies them ('A ransomware attack that has encrypted your core operational systems almost certainly meets this threshold'); 'Stage 3: Intermediate Report (If Requested)... If the competent authority or CSIRT requests it... you may need to provide a status update.'

expectations.text: Mentions parallel duties: informing recipients of services where relevant (Art. 23(1)-(2)) and the separate GDPR 72-hour personal data breach notification if personal data is affected
expectations.evidence: 'Article 23(1) also requires that, where appropriate, you notify the recipients of your services of significant incidents likely to adversely affect the provision of that service'; and under Parallel Obligations: 'GDPR (Article 33/34): If any personal data was encrypted, exfiltrated, or otherwise compromised... you likely have a separate 72-hour breach notification obligation to your Data Protection Authority.'
